qa_pairs.question: Whose responsibility is data migration to the new systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Data migration to the new systems is the responsibility of each National Host Site, who will inform users of what they need to do.

qa_pairs.question: Will my files be deleted during a complete data centre shutdown as part of renewal activities?
qa_pairs.answer: No, your files will not be deleted. During renewal activities, each National Host Site will migrate /project and /home data from the existing storage system to the new storage system once it is installed. Backups and /nearline data on tape systems will remain unchanged as these systems are not being replaced.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to /project and /home data during renewal activities?
qa_pairs.answer: During renewal activities, each National Host Site will migrate /project and /home data from the existing storage system to the new storage system once it is installed.

qa_pairs.question: Will tape systems for backups and /nearline data be replaced during the infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: No, tape systems for backups and /nearline data are not being replaced, so this data will remain unchanged.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I get further technical questions answered about renewal activities?
qa_pairs.answer: For further technical questions, you can email technical support, which goes directly to their ticketing system where a support expert can provide a detailed response.

qa_pairs.question: When can users expect outages to occur?
qa_pairs.answer: Each National Host Site will have its own schedule for outages as the installation of and transition to new equipment proceeds. Specific outages will be described on the system status web page ([https://status.alliancecan.ca]), with more general updates on the wiki page and periodic email notices.

qa_pairs.question: Where can specific outage details be found?
qa_pairs.answer: Specific outages will be described on the system status web page ([https://status.alliancecan.ca]).

qa_pairs.question: How will users receive general updates and outage notices?
qa_pairs.answer: General updates will be provided on this wiki page, and users will periodically receive emails with updates and outage notices.

qa_pairs.question: Whom should I contact for questions regarding the infrastructure transition?
qa_pairs.answer: You should contact technical support for any questions about the transition.

qa_pairs.question: Will existing jobs and applications be compatible with the new systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Generally yes, but the new CPUs and GPUs may require recompilation or reconfiguration of some applications. More details will be provided as the transition unfolds.

qa_pairs.question: What potential issues might arise for jobs and applications running on the new systems?
qa_pairs.answer: The new CPUs and GPUs may require recompilation or reconfiguration of some applications, though generally, jobs and applications should still be able to run.

qa_pairs.question: Will the standard software environment from the current systems be available on the new systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the standard software environment will be available on the new systems.

qa_pairs.question: Will commercial, licensed software be migrated to the new systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the plan is to transition current commercial software licenses to the new replacement systems, aiming for identical access to special applications (like Gaussian, AMS/ADF). However, there's a small risk of changes in licensing terms, which will be addressed individually.

qa_pairs.question: What is the plan for commercial software licenses during the transition?
qa_pairs.answer: The plan is to transition the current commercial software licenses from old systems to the new replacements, aiming for users to have identical access to special applications like Gaussian and AMS/ADF.

qa_pairs.question: Are staggered outages expected during the infrastructure renewal?
qa_pairs.answer: While best efforts will be made to limit overlapping outages, periods when several systems are simultaneously offline are probable due to constraints on delivery schedules and funding deadlines. Outages will be announced as early as possible.

qa_pairs.question: Why might multiple systems be offline simultaneously?
qa_pairs.answer: Multiple systems might be offline simultaneously due to constraints from delivery schedules and funding deadlines, even though efforts will be made to limit overlapping outages.

qa_pairs.question: Can users purchase old hardware after equipment upgrades?
qa_pairs.answer: Most of the equipment is the property of the hosting institution, which manages its disposal (typically e-cycling rather than selling) according to its guidelines. To inquire about acquiring old hardware, it's best to contact the host institution directly.

qa_pairs.question: How is retired equipment typically disposed of after upgrades?
qa_pairs.answer: Retired equipment is typically e-cycled (recycled) by the host institution following its disposal guidelines, rather than being sold.

qa_pairs.question: Who should be contacted if someone wants to acquire old hardware?
qa_pairs.answer: If you're looking to acquire old hardware, it's best to contact the host institution directly, as they may have specific policies or options for selling equipment.
